question: [File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: EcrecoverAuthorizer domain separator] Can an attacker under the precondition that EcrecoverAuthorizer uses block.chainid in the domain separator (not INITIAL_CHAIN_ID) submit a signature valid on chain A on chain B after a hard fork, violating the invariant that cross-chain signature replay is prevented, causing scoped impact: authorization replay across chains enabling unauthorized delegation on the forked chain? Proof idea: sign Authorization on chainId=1, simulate chainId change, assert signature is invalid on new chain due to domain separator mismatch.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee + take] Can the feeSetter under the precondition that active maker offers exist at low tick prices call setMarketSettlementFee(id, 1, maxSettlementFee(1)) to raise the settlement fee above the offer price, violating the invariant that existing offers should not be silently invalidated by fee changes, causing scoped impact: implicit offer cancellation via fee increase that makes buyerPrice > WAD or sellerPrice underflow, freezing taker flows? Proof idea: create offer at tick with price P, raise settlementFee to P+1, attempt take, assert it reverts due to arithmetic underflow in sellerPrice = offerPrice - settlementFee.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketContinuousFee + take] Can the feeSetter under the precondition that lenders have existing credit positions with pendingFee already accrued call setMarketContinuousFee(id, MAX_CONTINUOUS_FEE) to maximize the continuous fee, violating the invariant that existing lenders' pendingFee is fixed at take time and not retroactively increased, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where new takes accrue at max fee while existing positions retain old fee, creating inconsistent continuousFeeCredit? Proof idea: take at fee=0, setMarketContinuousFee(MAX), take again, assert old lender's pendingFee unchanged and new lender's pendingFee reflects new rate.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: claimContinuousFee + solvency] Can the feeClaimer under the precondition that continuousFeeCredit < withdrawable call claimContinuousFee(market, continuousFeeCredit, receiver), reducing withdrawable below the amount needed to cover all lender credits, violating the invariant that withdrawable >= sum of all lender credits, causing scoped impact: protocol insolvency where lenders cannot withdraw their full credit? Proof idea: invariant test asserting withdrawable >= sum(position[id][user].credit) for all users after any claimContinuousFee call.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setConsumed] Can an unprivileged caller (attacker) under the precondition that attacker is not authorized by victim call setConsumed(group, amount, victim), violating the invariant that only victim or victim's authorized agents can modify victim's consumed mapping, causing scoped impact: unauthorized offer cancellation or consumed manipulation enabling griefing of victim's active offers? Proof idea: fuzz caller != victim and !isAuthorized[victim][caller], assert setConsumed(group, amount, victim) reverts with Unauthorized().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setConsumed + take] Can an authorized operator under the precondition that isAuthorized[maker][operator]=true and maker has active offers call setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker) to cancel all offers, then immediately call setConsumed(group, 0, maker) to reset consumed, violating the invariant that consumed[user][group] must be monotonically non-decreasing, causing scoped impact: offer replay enabling previously cancelled offers to be filled again? Proof idea: setConsumed(group, type(uint256).max, maker), then setConsumed(group, 0, maker), assert second call reverts with AlreadyConsumed().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + withdrawCollateral] Can an authorized operator under the precondition that isAuthorized[borrower][operator]=true and borrower has collateral with debt call withdrawCollateral(market, index, assets, borrower, operator), violating the invariant that collateral withdrawal must leave the borrower healthy, causing scoped impact: unauthorized collateral seizure by operator draining borrower's collateral to operator's address? Proof idea: operator calls withdrawCollateral on borrower's behalf with receiver=operator, assert health check still applies and prevents unhealthy withdrawal.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + repay] Can an authorized operator under the precondition that isAuthorized[borrower][operator]=true call repay(market, units, borrower, callback, data) with a malicious callback that reenters setIsAuthorized to revoke operator's authorization mid-repay, violating the invariant that authorization state must not change during an active repay callback, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where repay completes but authorization is revoked before token pull? Proof idea: callback calls setIsAuthorized(operator, false, borrower), assert repay still completes and token is pulled from payer.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: multicall + setIsAuthorized] Can an attacker under the precondition that attacker is authorized by victim (isAuthorized[victim][attacker]=true) use multicall to atomically call [setIsAuthorized(attacker, false, victim), withdraw(market, units, victim, attacker)], violating the invariant that authorization revocation should prevent subsequent unauthorized actions in the same transaction, causing scoped impact: attacker withdraws victim's credit after revoking their own authorization to obscure the attack? Proof idea: multicall([setIsAuthorized(attacker, false, victim), withdraw(market, units, victim, attacker)]), assert withdraw reverts with Unauthorized() because authorization was revoked in first call.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee index boundary] Can the feeSetter under the precondition that a market exists call setMarketSettlementFee(id, 6, maxSettlementFee(6)) followed by setMarketSettlementFee(id, 7, 0), violating the invariant that index must be <= 6, causing scoped impact: out-of-bounds write to MarketState storage slot adjacent to settlementFeeCbp6 corrupting other market state fields? Proof idea: call setMarketSettlementFee(id, 7, 0), assert it reverts with InvalidFeeIndex().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee CBP rounding] Can the feeSetter under the precondition that a market exists call setMarketSettlementFee(id, index, fee) where fee % CBP != 0, violating the invariant that settlement fees must be multiples of CBP=1e12, causing scoped impact: fee stored as truncated uint16 cbp value that differs from intended fee, creating rounding discrepancy in claimableSettlementFee accounting? Proof idea: fuzz fee in [1, maxSettlementFee(index)] where fee % 1e12 != 0, assert call reverts with FeeNotMultipleOfFeeCbp().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: claimSettlementFee overclaim] Can the feeClaimer under the precondition that claimableSettlementFee[token] = X call claimSettlementFee(token, X+1, receiver), violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] >= amount must hold before transfer, causing scoped impact: underflow in claimableSettlementFee mapping enabling the feeClaimer to drain more tokens than accrued, creating protocol insolvency? Proof idea: claimSettlementFee(token, claimableSettlementFee[token]+1, receiver), assert it reverts due to arithmetic underflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setFeeClaimer + claimSettlementFee race] Can the roleSetter under the precondition that feeClaimer A has pending claimable fees call setFeeClaimer(B) atomically with B calling claimSettlementFee in the same block, violating the invariant that the old feeClaimer loses unclaimed fees when replaced, causing scoped impact: fee theft where new feeClaimer B claims fees that were accrued under old feeClaimer A's tenure? Proof idea: multicall([setFeeClaimer(B)]) from roleSetter, then B calls claimSettlementFee, assert B can claim all accumulated fees.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing + take] Can the tickSpacingSetter under the precondition that active offers exist at tick T where T % oldSpacing == 0 but T % newSpacing != 0 call setMarketTickSpacing(id, newSpacing), violating the invariant that tick spacing can only decrease (finer), causing scoped impact: existing offers at coarser ticks remain valid after refinement but new offers at those ticks are still accessible, enabling front-running? Proof idea: create offer at tick=4, setMarketTickSpacing(id, 2), assert offer at tick=4 still fillable (4 % 2 == 0), and offer at tick=2 now also fillable.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketTickSpacing increase attempt] Can the tickSpacingSetter under the precondition that current tickSpacing=1 attempt to call setMarketTickSpacing(id, 2), violating the invariant that tick spacing can only decrease (require(tickSpacingBefore % newTickSpacing == 0)), causing scoped impact: unauthorized tick spacing increase that would invalidate existing offers at odd ticks? Proof idea: setMarketTickSpacing(id, 1), then setMarketTickSpacing(id, 2), assert second call reverts with InvalidTickSpacing() since 1 % 2 != 0.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized self-authorization] Can an attacker under the precondition that attacker calls setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, attacker) authorize themselves for their own account, then use this to call setIsAuthorized(victim, true, attacker) on behalf of attacker (not victim), violating the invariant that self-authorization does not grant cross-account permissions, causing scoped impact: confusion between self-authorization and cross-account authorization enabling unauthorized actions? Proof idea: attacker calls setIsAuthorized(attacker, true, attacker), then calls setIsAuthorized(victim, true, attacker) with onBehalf=victim, assert second call reverts with Unauthorized().

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setDefaultSettlementFee + touchMarket race] Can an attacker under the precondition that feeSetter is about to call setDefaultSettlementFee(loanToken, index, highFee) front-run by calling touchMarket with that loanToken before the fee update, violating the invariant that market creation should inherit the current default fees, causing scoped impact: market created with zero or low fees that permanently avoids the intended fee schedule? Proof idea: touchMarket before setDefaultSettlementFee, assert market inherits zero fees; verify feeSetter must then call setMarketSettlementFee separately to fix the market.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + take (taker authorization)] Can an authorized operator under the precondition that isAuthorized[taker][operator]=true call take(offer, ratifierData, units, taker, receiver, callback, data) on behalf of taker, violating the invariant that taker authorization is not scoped by market or action type, causing scoped impact: operator can take any offer on behalf of taker in any market, creating unauthorized debt for taker? Proof idea: operator calls take with taker=victim, assert take succeeds and victim's debt increases, demonstrating coarse-grained authorization risk.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + liquidate] Can an authorized operator under the precondition that isAuthorized[borrower][operator]=true and borrower is healthy call liquidate(market, index, seizedAssets, repaidUnits, borrower, false, receiver, callback, data), violating the invariant that only unhealthy borrowers can be liquidated (NotLiquidatable check), causing scoped impact: operator cannot bypass health check to liquidate healthy borrower, but can they use authorization to manipulate borrower state to make them liquidatable? Proof idea: operator calls withdrawCollateral on borrower's behalf to reduce health, then liquidate, assert health check prevents liquidation if still healthy.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: multicall + claimSettlementFee] Can the feeClaimer under the precondition that claimableSettlementFee[token] = X use multicall to call claimSettlementFee(token, X, receiver) twice in the same transaction, violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee[token] must be >= amount before each claim, causing scoped impact: double-claim draining more tokens than accrued, creating protocol insolvency? Proof idea: multicall([claimSettlementFee(token, X, r), claimSettlementFee(token, 1, r)]), assert second call reverts due to underflow.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketSettlementFee + claimSettlementFee accounting] Can the feeSetter under the precondition that claimableSettlementFee[token] has accumulated from past takes call setMarketSettlementFee(id, 0, 0) to zero the post-maturity fee, then feeClaimer calls claimSettlementFee(token, claimableSettlementFee[token], receiver), violating the invariant that claimableSettlementFee must equal the sum of (buyerAssets - sellerAssets) across all takes, causing scoped impact: fee setter change does not retroactively alter claimableSettlementFee, but future takes at zero fee produce zero claimable, creating accounting desync? Proof idea: assert claimableSettlementFee[token] unchanged after setMarketSettlementFee, only future takes produce zero fee.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + flashLoan] Can an authorized operator under the precondition that isAuthorized[victim][operator]=true use flashLoan to borrow tokens and repay using victim's credit via a callback that calls withdraw(market, units, victim, operator), violating the invariant that flashLoan callbacks must not exploit authorization to drain third-party positions, causing scoped impact: unauthorized seizure of victim's credit via flashLoan + authorization combination? Proof idea: flashLoan callback calls withdraw(market, units, victim, operator) where operator is authorized, assert victim's credit decreases and operator receives tokens.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setFeeSetter + setMarketSettlementFee cross-market] Can the feeSetter under the precondition that multiple markets share the same loanToken call setDefaultSettlementFee(loanToken, index, newFee) and expect it to retroactively update all existing markets, violating the invariant that per-market fees are independent of default fees after market creation, causing scoped impact: feeSetter incorrectly assumes default fee change affects existing markets, leaving them with stale fees? Proof idea: create market M1, setDefaultSettlementFee(loanToken, 1, highFee), create market M2, assert M1 still has old fee and M2 has new fee.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setIsAuthorized + supplyCollateral poisoning] Can an authorized operator under the precondition that isAuthorized[borrower][operator]=true call supplyCollateral(market, index, assets, borrower) to activate a collateral with a reverting oracle for borrower, violating the invariant that supplyCollateral does not call the oracle but subsequent liquidate/isHealthy/withdrawCollateral will, causing scoped impact: operator poisons borrower's collateral bitmap with a reverting oracle, permanently freezing borrower's ability to withdraw collateral or be liquidated? Proof idea: operator calls supplyCollateral with reverting oracle collateral for borrower, assert subsequent liquidate and withdrawCollateral revert due to oracle revert.

[File: 'File Name: src/libraries/UtilsLib.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: setMarketContinuousFee + updatePosition] Can the feeSetter under the precondition that lenders have existing positions with pendingFee > 0 call setMarketContinuousFee(id, 0) to zero the continuous fee, then updatePosition is called for those lenders, violating the invariant that existing lenders' pendingFee is fixed at take time and not affected by fee changes, causing scoped impact: lenders with pre-existing pendingFee continue to accrue at old rate while new lenders accrue at zero, creating inconsistent fee accounting? Proof idea: take at fee=MAX, setMarketContinuousFee(0), updatePosition for old lender, assert pendingFee still accrues at old rate.
